text: Acute caffeine ingestion in moderate doses (3-6 mg/kg) produces a small but significant ergogenic effect on endurance time-trial performance.
Caffeine ingestion improves mean power output during endurance time-trials by approximately 3% compared to placebo.
Caffeine ingestion improves endurance time-trial completion time by approximately 2.2% compared to placebo.
Individual responses to caffeine vary, with some athletes experiencing slower performance or lower power output after caffeine ingestion.
Caffeine's ergogenic effect on endurance performance is small in magnitude (effect sizes 0.23-0.41) but can be meaningful in competitive settings where athletes are separated by small margins.
